name: Google Ads
one_liner: Google's online advertising platform for search, display, video, and shopping ads, exposing a full-featured API to manage campaigns, budgets, bidding, and reporting programmatically.
primary_auth: OAuth2 (user access/refresh tokens) plus a required developer token sent as a header
access_notes: Sign-up grants Test Account Access automatically (self-serve, 15,000 ops/day, but only works against test accounts). To call the API on real/production accounts you must apply for Basic Access via the API Center, which is a human-reviewed application (~5 business days). Standard Access (unlimited ops) requires already holding Basic and ~10 business days review. Thus any production API use is gated by approval, though a working test sandbox is self-serve.
api_notes: Comprehensive API (currently v20) covering campaigns, ad groups, ads, keywords, budgets, bidding strategies, audiences, conversions, and reporting via GAQL (Google Ads Query Language). Available over gRPC (preferred) and REST/JSON. Google ships official client libraries for Java, Python, .NET, PHP, Ruby, and Perl. 100+ resources/services.
mcp_notes: Google ships an official open-source Google Ads MCP server (github.com/googleads/google-ads-mcp), documented at developers.google.com. It is self-hosted (run locally over stdio or deploy to Cloud Run), strictly read-only, exposing three tools: list_accessible_customers, search (GAQL reporting), and get_resource_metadata. Write operations remain API-only. Community MCP servers (GoMarble, DigitalRocket, etc.) also exist.
main_blocker: Developer token Basic Access approval (~5 business days) is required before the API works against real advertiser accounts; only test accounts are self-serve. Auth also requires combining OAuth2 tokens with a developer token and login-customer-id headers.

evidence.claim: Google Ads API uses OAuth 2.0 (scope https://www.googleapis.com/auth/adwords) plus a required 22-character developer token from the API Center.

Developer tokens have Test, Basic, and Standard access levels; Test is granted automatically at sign-up (test accounts only), while Basic/Standard require an application reviewed over ~5-10 business days.
Google maintains an official open-source, read-only Google Ads MCP server exposing list_accessible_customers, search, and get_resource_metadata.
The developer token is required for all API calls and its access level controls daily call limits and test vs production environments.

open_questions: Whether Basic Access approval is routinely granted to small/individual developers or effectively harder for agent-tool platforms. Exact ops/resource count for BROAD not enumerated but clearly well over 100.
